atDirectives.name: @apply
atDirectives.description: Use the `@apply` directive to inline any existing utility classes into your own custom CSS.

atDirectives.name: @config
atDirectives.description: Use the @config directive to load a legacy JavaScript-based configuration file.

atDirectives.name: @custom-variant
atDirectives.description: Use the @custom-variant directive to add a custom variant in your project.

atDirectives.name: @import
atDirectives.description: Use the @import directive to inline import CSS files, including Tailwind itself.

atDirectives.name: @layer
atDirectives.description: Use the @layer directive to tell Tailwind which 'bucket' a set of custom styles belong to. Valid layers are a base, components, and utilities.

atDirectives.name: @plugin
atDirectives.description: Use the @plugin directive to load a legacy JavaScript-based plugin.

atDirectives.name: @reference
atDirectives.description: If you want to use @apply or @variant in the <style> block of a Vue or Svelte component, or within CSS modules, you will need to import your theme variables, custom utilities, and custom variants to make those values available in that context.

atDirectives.name: @responsive
atDirectives.description: You can generate responsive variants of your own classes by wrapping their definitions in the `@responsive` directive.

atDirectives.name: @screen
atDirectives.description: The `@screen` directive allows you to create media queries that reference your breakpoints by **name** instead of duplicating their values in your own CSS.

atDirectives.name: @source
atDirectives.description: Use the `@source` directive to explicitly specify source files that aren't picked up by Tailwind's automatic content detection.

atDirectives.name: @tailwind
atDirectives.description: Use the `@tailwind` directive to insert Tailwind's `base`, `components`, `utilities` and `screens` styles into your CSS.

atDirectives.name: @theme
atDirectives.description: Use the `@theme` directive to define your project's custom design tokens, like fonts, colors, and breakpoints.

atDirectives.name: @utility
atDirectives.description: Use the `@utility` directive to add custom utilities to your project that work with variants like `hover`, `focus` and `lg`.

atDirectives.name: @variant
atDirectives.description: Use the @variant directive to apply a Tailwind variant to styles in your CSS.

atDirectives.name: @variants
atDirectives.description: Generate `hover`, `focus`, `active` and other **variants** of your own utilities by wrapping their definitions in the `@variants` directive.
